title: MOTION PICTURE
wikitext:
{{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:MOTION PICTURE }} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=MotionPicture.png|bg-size=500px|text-color=rgb(210,220,210)|class=0|entity=Devoid of Entities|hexagon=0}} '''MOTION PICTURE''' is an anomalous location within the backrooms. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=MotionPicture Lobby.jpg|caption=Should I even be here...?|align=Left}} I push open the dirty glass doors. It's dusty in here. There are slots for posters on the walls, but they've rotted past recognition. Fancy booths and arcade machines long rusted over are scattered around the plaza. Seems like a movie theater. It's so dark, and everything is so still. It feels wrong just to disturb this place. The silence is so loud, I almost prefer the incessant buzzing of fluorescent lights. At least their sound was somewhat of a distraction from the total isolation. Against my better judgement, I progress, towards the counter. Everything's trashed, rotted, a level of decay on which the beam of my flashlight barely scratches the surface. Somehow, this place seems more... personal than the others. I look beyond the counter, but if there ever were any snacks there, they're long gone now. The coolers are dead silent, the popcorn machines dusty and shattered. But there's a single paper ticket on the counter, almost pristine in nature, that sticks out like a sore thumb. It's colored entirely black except for the words "MOTION PICTURE" printed in white. No date, no time, no theater number or brand. I pick it up and put it in my pocket. Why not? A memento would be nice. I've been through so many of these [[Level List|places]], met so many [[Persons of Interest|people]], run from so many [[Entity List|monsters]], it's all begun to blur. My enthusiasm, my drive to escape and survive, it's just not what it's used to be. Despite my best efforts, I've gotten old. But I try not to think about it too much. I try to tell myself, when I lie awake at night, haunted by the sounds of distant horrors, that I'll make it [[The Frontrooms|home]] one day. That's why I never settled down. Just couldn't accept it, I guess. Does that make me foolish? Probably. Everybody else seems to think so. It's partly that, at least. Fear that not only settling here would mean accepting my fate, but also that it might be for nothing. Who knows if I'd be ripped away from these people again, the same way I was taken from my family? My friends? My whole world? I don't know how the others can stand it. I've seen it happen again and again. This whole place is a vicious cycle. So I have to keep moving. I can't give up. I just can't give up. Navigating through the darkened halls, my feet crunch against old garbage and crusty carpeting. If I hadn't seen the lobby, I probably wouldn't even be able to tell what kind of building this was. The theater rooms seem open, but they're all dark, and I'm not interested in the cheap nostalgia of the environment. Then I turn a corner, and I see a light. Past one of the cinema doors, there's a screen on. My curiosity gets the better of me. It's been so long since I've seen any kind of media, any book, game or movie, any distraction from the hell my life has become. Running, exploring, enduring, never stopping to catch my breath. The idea is so tempting. Maybe I could indulge, just for a moment. {{Blurimage|file=MotionPicture.png|caption=Now showing.|align=Right}} I push the rusty doors open with some difficulty, their creaks echoing loudly, and make my way toward the screen. It's showing a dining room, but not like the ones I've seen here. This one has life. A family sits around the table. There's no sounds. I guess the speakers are broken- I'm surprised the projector isn't too. The family talks. Then a woman calls out to someone. They walk into the room, sitting down in their chair. I'm no lip reader, but maybe they apologized? It takes me a moment, but I recognize the person. They're me. But they look so young, so unburdened. Talking to people I've never met. A family? It must be some sick trick, I think to myself. It certainly wouldn't be the first I've seen. What a mockery, to show me the life I could have had, if only I never came here, doomed eternally to wander these endless halls. Look at them, on the screen, so idyllic. They yell and they argue, yet they know not of true hardship. Of true loneliness. Not like I do. But as I watch the screen, as I watch myself continue to live, to do the things I never got to do, as I stand alone in the dark, a primal fear starts to burn inside me. A fear stronger than that of my own death. What if? What if it's not a trick? What if that's really me out there, living? What if I never left my home? What if I'm the fake one? == Entities == It's only you. == Colonies & Outposts == Why would anyone want to live here? == Entrances == * Enter a derelict movie theater in [[Level 11]]. * Enter a random door in [[Level 156]]. * == Exits == * Wander far enough and you will find yourself in the [[The Hub|Hub]]. {{Collapse|num=3|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body= Page written by [[User:SlenderTales|SlenderTales]]
Pictures from [https://www.reddit.com/r/Urbex/comments/1bil1ui/abandoned_movie_theatre_in_cobb_county_ga/ Reddit].{{User:SlenderTales/MyPages}}}} {{Blurbackground|file=MotionPicture Lobby.jpg|opacity=0.5}} __NOTOC__ __NOEDITSECTION__ [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Anomalous Levels]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Tales]] [[Category:Secret Levels]] [[Category:Darkness]] [[Category:Horror]] [[Category:Rare]]
